roles.role: Director of Data Science, Performance Marketing
roles.note: YOUR #1 ACTIVE ROLE and arguably the best of the whole search. Director title = the scope step-up you want, and the mandate is a bullseye on your wedge: lead the DS team running Root's performance-marketing engine — owning a NINE-FIGURE marketing budget, LTV-based bidding, incrementality/MMM measurement, experimentation. This is monetization + growth + experimentation + 0→1 leadership, exactly your story. Warm INBOUND from the hiring manager (Annie Yang sourced you on LinkedIn), not a cold app — a strong signal. Honest caveats: (1) company trajectory — Root is a small-cap public insurtech, but it just turned profitable (sub-100% combined ratio, record Q1'26), so the story is improving; (2) JD says PhD-preferred + full-funnel paid-acquisition experience — manage that gap by leaning on quant depth + actuarial-science degree + experimentation/monetization work. Frame everything as marketing spend → LTV-positive (profitable-risk) policies, not raw volume.

app.note: ADVANCED 7/2: invited to the next round — a 60-min video interview with the hiring manager (Annie Yang), technical + discussion-based on experience, problem-solving, and core concepts, with a few APPLIED questions (have a notebook ready). Kristin Simmons sent a prep guide for the full process and asked for availability over the next 10 days. ACTION: send availability promptly; prep with root_annie_prep.html — lead with LTV-positive acquisition, incrementality/MMM, nine-figure budget allocation; refresh stats/predictive modeling (GLMs, causal) that Annie values over LLMs.

roles.tier: Consumer fintech · subsidiary of Upbound Group (NASDAQ: UPBD)
roles.note: NEW FIND (LinkedIn sweep 7/8). Director of DS at Brigit — a consumer financial-health app (instant cash advances, credit-building, subscription) that Upbound Group (NASDAQ: UPBD, ex-Rent-A-Center) acquired Jan 2025 for up to $460M. Brigit is GROWING and profitable-adjacent: ~1M+ paying subscribers, ~$215–230M rev in 2025, guided to ~$350–400M in 2026, now backed by a public parent (stability + resources). WEDGE BULLSEYE: consumer fintech, subscription monetization, credit/lending, customer-lifecycle + growth — exactly your story. Director title = the scope step-up you want, and an SF role for an NYC-HQ company likely means building/leading a new West-Coast DS footprint (0→1 team-building, your strength). CAVEATS: (1) it's now a subsidiary of Upbound — some prestige discount vs a pure venture fintech, though the financials are stronger for it; (2) apps are managed off LinkedIn, and there's a $60M 2026 earnout on Brigit's performance, so the org is under real growth pressure — frame yourself as the monetization/growth leader who hits those numbers.
